message.content:
You are verifier #1, independent. Do NOT optimistically confirm. Read the ACTUAL code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay and decide if this is a TRUE production-readiness problem in CURRENT code (4 hardening passes applied). Confirmed=true ONLY if the problem exists as described, the PoC reproduces, and it is genuine (not speculative/already-handled/by-design). Cite file:line.

H-018: Operator-username PII not redacted from transcripts when the home-dir name is short (<4 chars) or appears in a dash-delimited project slug
File: scripts/capture_transcripts.py:67
Description: _scrub redacts the operator username two ways: the path rule '(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s"']+' (requires a literal '/Users/[REDACTED]') and _USERNAME_RE derived from Path.home().name. The Claude project-dir slug is dash-delimited (e.g. '-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc'); the path rule does NOT match it (no slashes), so the only mask inside slugs/git-author strings/prose is _USERNAME_RE — which returns None for any home-dir name shorter than 4 chars (to avoid over-redacting common words). For operators whose login is <4 chars, the username then leaks verbatim into committed transcripts via the slug and prose, defeating the stated #F-024/#G-007 PII-scrubbing goal. The docstring claims operator PII is redacted, so this is an undisclosed completeness gap.
PoC: Set HOME so Path.home().name='bob'; create $SRC=.../-Users-bob-hc-grc/<id>.jsonl containing the slug path and 'bob'. Run capture_transcripts — the committed transcript still contains 'bob' and '-Users-bob-...' because _USERNAME_RE is None (len<4) and the '/Users/' rule never matches the dash slug.
Suggested fix: Always derive the slug form of the home path (replace os.sep with '-') and redact it explicitly regardless of username length; lower/remove the len<4 skip in favor of word-boundary-anchored token redaction. At minimum document that usernames <4 chars are NOT auto-redacted so reviewers scrub before publishing.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Capture the active Claude session's JSONL transcript(s) into the repo for provenance.
3	
4	Run by the pre-commit hook (.githooks/pre-commit) so every commit pulls in an up-to-date
5	snapshot of the agent interactions/effort behind it — an auditable record alongside the work.
6	
7	What it captures: the *active* session (the most-recently-modified top-level transcript in the
8	Claude project dir) plus that session's ``<id>/`` subtree (subagents + workflow transcripts).
9	Other/unrelated sessions are left out. Credential-shaped secrets are redacted on the way in.
10	
11	Honest scope: transcripts still contain local filesystem paths and may contain the operator's
12	email and internal reasoning. If this repo is published, review/exclude ``transcripts/`` first
13	(see transcripts/README.md). The wheel never ships them (only ``src/assay_engine`` is packaged).
14	
15	Config: ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC overrides the source dir. Exits 0 on any error (never blocks a
16	commit).
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime as _dt
22	import hashlib
23	import json
24	import os
25	import re
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	DEFAULT_SRC = Path.home() / ".claude" / "projects" / "-Users-[REDACTED]-hc-grc"
30	DEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "sessions"
31	MANIFEST = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "transcripts" / "MANIFEST.json"
32	
33	# Credential-shaped secrets to redact (defense). Pass 3 (#F-024) extends this to operator PII —
34	# email addresses and the home-directory username embedded in filesystem paths — so committing
35	# transcripts as provenance (the campaign mandate) does not also publish the operator's identity.
36	# Reasoning content and the path STRUCTURE remain (legitimate provenance); see the README caveat
37	# for the full public-release consideration.
38	_SECRET_PATTERNS = [
39	    re.compile(r"sk-ant-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{2,}"),  # any sk-ant- token incl. oat01 OAuth + placeholders
40	    re.compile(r"\bgh[posru]_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"),  # GitHub tokens
41	    re.compile(r"\bAKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}\b"),  # AWS access key id
42	    # bearer tokens incl. base64url/base64 JWTs — must cover + / = or the token is split at the
43	    # first such char and only the leading fragment is redacted (pass 3, #F-051).
44	    re.compile(r"(?i)bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-+/=]{20,}"),
45	    # explicit OAuth/token env values: "...OAUTH_TOKEN":"<value>" / ...KEY=<value>
46	    re.compile(r'((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)"\s*:\s*")[^"]{8,}(")'),
47	    re.compile(r"((?:OAUTH_TOKEN|_TOKEN|_KEY|_SECRET)=)[^\s\"']{8,}"),
48	    # operator PII (#F-024): email addresses, and the username segment of a home path
49	    # (/Users/[REDACTED] /home/[REDACTED] — the path STRUCTURE is kept, only the identity is masked.
50	    re.compile(r"\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}\b"),  # email → [REDACTED]
51	    re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+"),  # /Users/[REDACTED] → /Users/[REDACTED] _username_pattern() -> re.Pattern[str] | None:
56	    """A redaction for the bare operator username wherever it appears (#G-007).
57	
58	    The /Users|/home path rule only masks the username inside a path; the same handle leaks via
59	    git author strings, prose, and the Claude project-dir slug (``-Users-<name>-...``). Derive the
60	    handle from the home directory and redact it as a standalone token too. Skipped for trivially
61	    short/None handles (e.g. a CI 'root') to avoid over-redacting common words.
62	    """
63	    try:
64	        name = Path.home().name
65	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 - never break capture over a missing home
66	        return None
67	    if not name or len(name) < 4:
68	        return None
69	    return re.compile(re.escape(name))
70	
71	
72	_USERNAME_RE = _username_pattern()
73	
74	
75	def _scrub(text: str) -> str:
76	    for pat in _SECRET_PATTERNS:
77	        if pat.groups == 2:
78	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]\2", text)
79	        elif pat.groups == 1:
80	            text = pat.sub(r"\1[REDACTED]", text)
81	        else:
82	            text = pat.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
83	    # Redact the bare operator username everywhere (#G-007), after the path rule so masked paths
84	    # don't re-expose it. Done last so it also catches it inside project-dir slugs / git authors.
85	    if _USERNAME_RE is not None:
86	        text = _USERNAME_RE.sub("[REDACTED]", text)
87	    return text
88	
89	
90	def _active_session(src: Path) -> Path | None:
91	    """The most-recently-modified top-level transcript = the live session."""
92	    candidates = sorted(src.glob("*.jsonl"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)
93	    return candidates[0] if candidates else None
94	
95	
96	def _copy_scrubbed(srcf: Path, dstf: Path) -> dict | None:
97	    try:
98	        dstf.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
99	        raw = srcf.read_bytes()
100	        if srcf.suffix == ".jsonl":
101	            data = _scrub(raw.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")).encode("utf-8")
102	        else:
103	            data = raw
104	        if dstf.exists() and dstf.read_bytes() == data:
105	            pass  # unchanged — still report it in the manifest
106	        else:
107	            dstf.write_bytes(data)
108	        return {
109	            "path": str(dstf.relative_to(DEST.parent.parent)),
110	            "bytes": len(data),
111	            "sha256": hashlib.sha256(data).hexdigest(),
112	        }
113	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — provenance capture must never break a commit
114	        return None
115	
116	
117	def main() -> int:
118	    src = Path(os.environ.get("ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC", str(DEFAULT_SRC)))
119	    if not src.is_dir():
120	        return 0
121	    session = _active_session(src)
122	    if session is None:
123	        return 0
124	    sid = session.stem
125	    captured: list[dict] = []
126	
127	    rec = _copy_scrubbed(session, DEST / f"{sid}.jsonl")
128	    if rec:
129	        captured.append(rec)
130	
131	    # the session's subtree: subagents/ + workflows/ transcripts
132	    subtree = src / sid
133	    if subtree.is_dir():
134	        for f in sorted(subtree.rglob("*")):
135	            if f.is_file():
136	                rel = f.relative_to(subtree)
137	                rec = _copy_scrubbed(f, DEST / sid / rel)
138	                if rec:
139	                    captured.append(rec)
140	
141	    try:
142	        MANIFEST.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
143	        MANIFEST.write_text(
144	            json.dumps(
145	                {
146	                    "captured_at": _dt.datetime.now(tz=_dt.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
147	                    "active_session": sid,
148	                    "n_files": len(captured),
149	                    "total_bytes": sum(c["bytes"] for c in captured),
150	                    "files": captured,
151	                },
152	                indent=2,
153	            )
154	            + "\n",
155	            encoding="utf-8",
156	        )
157	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001
158	        return 0
159	    print(f"captured {len(captured)} transcript file(s) for session {sid}")
160	    return 0
161	
162	
163	if __name__ == "__main__":
164	    sys.exit(main())
165

content.text: The code matches the description exactly. Let me verify the PoC behavior empirically.

input.command:
cd /tmp && rm -rf h018test && mkdir -p h018test/home/[REDACTED]/projects/-Users-bob-hc-grc && export HOME=/tmp/h018test/home
cat > /tmp/h018test/sess.jsonl <<'EOF'
{"path":"/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/file.py","slug":"-Users-bob-hc-grc","author":"bob","prose":"the operator bob did this"}
EOF
export ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC=/tmp/h018test/home/[REDACTED]/projects/-Users-bob-hc-grc
cp /tmp/h018test/sess.jsonl "$ASSAY_TRANSCRIPT_SRC/abc123.jsonl"
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && python3 scripts/capture_transcripts.py
echo "=== captured file ==="
cat /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/transcripts/sessions/abc123.jsonl
echo "=== cleanup ==="
rm -f /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/transcripts/sessions/abc123.jsonl

content.content:
captured 1 transcript file(s) for session abc123
=== captured file ===
{"path":"/Users/[REDACTED]/hc-grc/file.py","slug":"-Users-bob-hc-grc","author":"bob","prose":"the operator bob did this"}
=== cleanup ===

content.text:
The PoC reproduces exactly. With `HOME` set so `Path.home().name == 'bob'` (3 chars, <4):

- The `/Users/[REDACTED]/` path is redacted to `/Users/[REDACTED]/` (path rule works).
- But the slug `-Users-bob-hc-grc`, the git-author `bob`, and the prose `the operator bob did this` ALL leak `bob` verbatim because `_USERNAME_RE` is `None` (len 3 < 4).

Let me restore the git working tree to clean and verify the docstring claim about PII redaction.

input.command: cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && git checkout transcripts/MANIFEST.json && git status --short

content.text:
The `sess1.jsonl` was just transient. Tree is now clean (the earlier `git checkout` reverted MANIFEST.json). My test artifacts are cleaned up.

The finding is confirmed:

- Code at `scripts/capture_transcripts.py:67` — `if not name or len(name) < 4: return None` skips username redaction for handles <4 chars.
- The path rule at line 51 `(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s"']+` only matches slash-delimited paths, never the dash slug.
- Docstring/comments (lines 11, 34-35, 56-61) claim operator PII/username is redacted.
- PoC reproduced: with `bob` (3 chars), the slug `-Users-bob-hc-grc`, git-author `bob`, and prose `bob` all leaked verbatim into the committed transcript, while only the `/Users/[REDACTED]/` path form was masked.

This is a genuine, undisclosed completeness gap defeating the stated #F-024/#G-007 PII-scrubbing goal for operators with short usernames. Severity is medium: it requires a specific precondition (operator login name <4 chars), and reasoning/path-structure is intentionally retained anyway with a documented public-release caveat — but the contradiction between the explicit "username redacted everywhere (#G-007)" claim and actual behavior is a real privacy-completeness defect.

input.reasoning: Confirmed in current code and reproduced via PoC. scripts/capture_transcripts.py:67 (`if not name or len(name) < 4: return None`) disables _USERNAME_RE for home-dir names shorter than 4 chars. The only other username mask is the path rule at line 51, `re.compile(r"(/(?:Users|home)/)[^/\s\"']+")`, which requires slash-delimited `/Users/[REDACTED] and never matches the dash-delimited Claude project slug `-Users-<name>-...`, git-author strings, or prose. PoC: HOME set so Path.home().name='bob' (3 chars); a transcript containing `/Users/[REDACTED]/...`, slug `-Users-bob-hc-grc`, author `bob`, and prose `the operator bob did this` was run through capture_transcripts.py. Output: only `/Users/[REDACTED]/` was masked; `-Users-bob-hc-grc`, `bob` (author), and `the operator bob did this` all leaked verbatim into the committed transcript. This contradicts the explicit claims in the docstring/comments (lines 11, 34-35 "operator PII... username embedded in filesystem paths"; lines 56-61 and 83-84 claiming the bare username is "redact[ed]... everywhere" via #G-007 "wherever it appears... project-dir slug"). It is an undisclosed completeness gap defeating #F-024/#G-007 for operators with logins <4 chars. Severity is medium rather than high: it requires a specific precondition (operator login name <4 chars — the campaign operator is '[REDACTED]', 11 chars, so not currently exposed), transcripts are not shipped in the wheel, and the README already carries a general public-release review caveat. But the gap is real, the in-code claim of universal username redaction is false for short handles, and the skip is silent/undocumented at the failure point.

content.text: Confirmed H-018 as a true medium-severity finding. The username-redaction skip for handles <4 chars (line 67) combined with the slash-only path rule (line 51) means short operator logins leak verbatim through dash-delimited slugs, git-author strings, and prose — contradicting the in-code claim of universal username redaction. PoC reproduced cleanly; test artifacts cleaned up and working tree restored.
